title: Copy Adjacent Lines
subtitle: {key:Ctrl-Y} and {key:Ctrl-E} pull from above and below.
summary: Ctrl-Y copies the character directly above the cursor; Ctrl-E copies the character directly below. Surprisingly handy for aligned text.

blocks.text: Two of *insert mode*'s most under-used keys: {key:Ctrl-Y} and {key:Ctrl-E}. Each one copies a single character from the line above or below the cursor — at the same column.

blocks.title: Built for Repetitive Text
blocks.text:
These were added to make duplicating column-aligned content quick — think duplicating a similar variable name, repeating a comment marker, or copying the same prefix across lines. Each press copies one character at the cursor's *column*. Hold them down and you're effectively duplicating the line above or below, character by character.

The mnemonic: {key:Ctrl-Y} for *Y*ank from above, {key:Ctrl-E} for *E*cho from below. Or just remember: {key:Ctrl-Y}/{key:Ctrl-E} also scroll up/down in *normal mode* — same direction, opposite "feel."
If the line above is shorter than the cursor's column, {key:Ctrl-Y} does nothing (no character to copy).
